name: Gut acid/pH sensor (GadE + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Reports gut acidity / low pH (linked to short-chain-fatty-acid load and dysbiosis). The acid-responsive Gad system gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the food-grade acid-tolerant chassis Lactococcus lactis.

input.detects: Luminal acidity (low pH from short-chain fatty acids / fermentation)

sensing.sensorMechanism: The GadE/GadX acid-response activator switches on glutamate-dependent acid-resistance promoters (PgadA/PgadB) at low pH.
sensing.signalFlow: Low pH -> GadE activates PgadA -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises as the environment acidifies.

parts.name: GadE/GadX acid regulator
parts.source: E. coli glutamate-dependent acid resistance (gad regulon)
parts.notes: Acid-responsive activator; requires porting/adapting for L. lactis (itself acid-tolerant).

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak reporter promoter

performance.notes: The Gad acid response and CRISPRa are validated separately; integrated device is a design template. L. lactis is natively acid-tolerant, suiting low-pH gut/food settings.

safety.biocontainment: thyA-based containment in L. lactis; food-grade host.
safety.notes: GRAS / food-grade chassis; research / supervised use.

buildSteps.title: Adapt + design
buildSteps.description: Adapt the acid-responsive promoter for L. lactis; design the CRISPRa sgRNA.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: GadE + PgadA -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9-omega. TU3: weak promoter -> mCherry. NICE vectors.

provenance.source: Design template combining the Gad acid-response module with L. lactis CRISPRa
partsSources: Foster / E. coli glutamate-dependent acid resistance (GadE/GadX)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
